post_rendered:
Mafia Game 235
Adventure for Creativity

  • Rules
    • Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • Do not post at night.
    • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • Day chat will be enabled via QuickTopic.
    • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • Do not imitate the mods.
  • Voting/Hanging
    • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Type usernames exactly. There is one exception, l and I are treated as the same.
    • Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though you have the ability to do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • If the deadline is met the player with the most votes is hanged. If there is a tie the player who received the votes first is hanged. If No Hang is part of the tie, No Hang occurs.
    • Starting day 2, If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • Starting day 2, If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • The moment the deadline passes, it will be twilight and no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • Once I (or the comod) post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • Each day will be 240 hours (10 days). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion.
    • The deadlines will be based on Central Timezone.
    • The deadline will occur the very second 240 hours has occurred.
    • The night deadline will be 48 hours after night begins.
    • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and they are not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period and they are not in V/LA, they will receive a PM prod.
    • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and they are not in V/LA, or a player has received too many PM prods, they will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • If a player is going to be V/LA, they must PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • Do not use inappropriate language or make personal attacks.
    • Be mature.
    • The game is to not be considered over until the mod or co-mod declares it as such.
    • Don't intentionally/purposely gamethrow. If you believe you can no longer win talk to me in your role pm.
    • For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after they have run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, they may be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • I (NDMath) am the moderator of Mafia Game 235. falliinqsnow- is the comod of Mafia Game 235
    • This brown (#974E03) is my mod color. Do not post in anything too similar. This light blue (#2BADF2) is falliingsnow-'s mod color. Do not post in anything too similar.
    • The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules. The co-mod the second to last say.
    • Mafia fake claims will be treated symmetrically to town roles.

  • Read all of the rules
Story
The Goddess Snow needed some more creativity and motivation, so she gathered a group of people and asked for them to help her. The thirteen of them all agreed (free will is a myth) to look for some, after a bit of debate and uncertainty. Before they left, a few gathered some 'resources' beforehand and then they headed towards the woods, which was the vague direction where Goddess Snow had waved at.

Only a few steps into the forest, everyone started to feel the creeping sensation that this was a very odd place. Like a normal forest, there were lots of trees, undergrowth, moss, some animals, and other life. But what made everyone the most uncomfortable was the sounds that echoed from tree to tree. The noises were mostly created by animals that made many different, odd noises never heard before, and sudden movements were spotted occasionally as the group traversed onward. It was obvious that this would be a scary place if the group was still inside it by nightfall or not in a safe place to rest. The group traveled over a worn out and unused trail, with many leaves scattered over it and undergrowth already poking out of the ground, making there more green than actual path-like paving. Repeatedly, branches hung over the path with various heights, making the group duck under them ever so often.

After several hours, some of the group is certain that they have passed that particular tree with the sideways branch for the third time. The group quickly comes to the conclusion that they're all walking in a big circle there must be sabotage from within. Accusations quickly start being tossed, and there was also the decision of who to abandon before nightfall–all during the very daring adventure for creativity.


Setup
The setup is closed, but the alignment ratio is 10 town and 3 mafia.

This game uses the Limited Set and meets all the requirements detailed in that thread relevant for a 10:3 limited game.

^That link is by far the most important in terms of set up information.

The action resolution for this game is Reasonable Action Resolution.
By default, all mafia are multi-tasking, meaning they may do more than one action on a single night. Private Chats are via QuickTopic with daychat enabled.

There is no flavor inside role PMs.

ad333 wrote:
Hi here's some stuff I copied. Automatic ISO Code
Written by casi and improved by Mathletesv. Reposted with permission.

ISO Code
Number.prototype.between = function(a, b, inclusive) {
  var min = Math.min(a, b),
    max = Math.max(a, b);
min -=1, max += 1;
 
  return inclusive ? this >= min && this <= max : this > min && this < max;
}
function number_matches(quote_box,start,end) {
return (parseInt($(quote_box).parents(".cmty-post-tr").children(".cmty-post-middle").children(".cmty-post-top-inline.cmty-post-top-data").children(".cmty-post-top").children(".cmty-post-number").text().slice(1)).between(start,end) )
    }
function user_matches (quote_box,username) {
    return ($(quote_box).parent().parent().children(".cmty-post-left").children(".cmty-post-username").text() == username)
    }

user = prompt("What is the username of the person you are iso'ing?");
min = parseInt(prompt("First number of range"), 10);
max = parseInt(prompt("Second number of range"), 10);
$(".cmty-post-quote").each(function () {
    if (user_matches(this, user) && number_matches(this, min, max)) {
        $(this).click()
    }
    })


Source


Description: given a username and a range of posts to look in, this code will automatically quote all posts from the specified player into the reply field.

Note: this code does not work on Android, iOS, or any other device without a javascript web console.

Instructions
Step 1: Load the posts you want to ISO.
The code cannot retrieve a player’s posts if you do not have them currently loaded in your browser, so you must scroll down (or up) through the thread to load the posts you want in your ISO. AoPS periodically refuses to load posts when scrolling up; if this happens, “bounce” the thread by scrolling down for a bit before scrolling back up.
Step 2: Open your browser’s console.
For Chrome, the shortcut is Control + Shift + J (Windows, Linux, Chrome OS) or Command + Option + J (macOS). For Safari, first enable Developer Menu by going to Safari Menu > Preferences > Advanced tab and checking “Show Develop menu in menu bar,” then use the shortcut Command + Option + C. For Firefox, the shortcut is Control + Shift + K (Windows, Linux) or Command + Option + K (macOS). For Microsoft Edge, the shortcut is Control + Shift + J.
Step 3: Copy/paste the code into the console and hit Enter.

After a whole day of resentful accusing during another few dozen of rounds of seeing very similar looking trees, many were hateful towards each other and tensions occurred, everyone believing that someone was deliberately interfering with the already hard and mind-blowing creativity adventure. Various accidents of fights between one and another occurred, and many accusations were tossed as tensions grew higher than ever. In fact, not one single person had their mind on the original task–all effort was either spent on trying to find the saboteurs or trying to hide the fact that they were indeed one.

Finally, as the sun almost set, the group came to a stop under a few particularly leafy trees, in hope that they would protect them from rain and other dangers of the forest. The ground was filled with undergrowth, and bushes spotted the area with ripe berries on them. Surveying their surroundings, the group quickly moved over to a more bare patch of ground in the middle of this safe haven.

Yet before anyone even considered going to sleep or gathering any materials for the long journey, they decided that somebody had to be kicked out for the safety and success of the mission. Although it was a rather painful decision for almost everyone, and all feared being left alone, they had accepted it and now they had to make the final decision. Despite that during the trek many hostilities had formed, when it was time for the voting, it was a clear and easy decision for most.

‣ Yet it seems like the town was fooled. ad333 was told to leave immediately and start trekking back. They were a Vanilla Townie.

Gloomy about abandoning the wrong person, everyone grumpily formed some makeshift shelter to rest in. In their distrust, everyone slept as far away from each other as possible, but maybe that won't even be enough...


Night deadline is July 5th at 9:50 pm Central. Day 2 will start sometime after that.
(Night will be extended if a replacement is needed for ostriches.)

After a whole day of resentful accusing during another few dozen of rounds of seeing very similar looking trees, many were hateful towards each other and tensions occurred, everyone believing that someone was deliberately interfering with the already hard and mind-blowing creativity adventure. Various accidents of fights between one and another occurred, and many accusations were tossed as tensions grew higher than ever. In fact, not one single person had their mind on the original task–all effort was either spent on trying to find the saboteurs or trying to hide the fact that they were indeed one.

Finally, as the sun almost set, the group came to a stop under a few particularly leafy trees, in hope that they would protect them from rain and other dangers of the forest. The ground was filled with undergrowth, and bushes spotted the area with ripe berries on them. Surveying their surroundings, the group quickly moved over to a more bare patch of ground in the middle of this safe haven.

Yet before anyone even considered going to sleep or gathering any materials for the long journey, they decided that somebody had to be kicked out for the safety and success of the mission. Although it was a rather painful decision for almost everyone, and all feared being left alone, they had accepted it and now they had to make the final decision. Despite that during the trek many hostilities had formed, when it was time for the voting, it was a clear and easy decision for most.

‣ Yet it seems like the town was fooled. ad333 was told to leave immediately and start trekking back. They were a [color=#0f0]Vanilla Townie[/color].

Gloomy about abandoning the wrong person, everyone grumpily formed some makeshift shelter to rest in. In their distrust, everyone slept as far away from each other as possible, but maybe that won't even be enough...


Night deadline is July 5th at 9:50 pm Central. Day 2 will start sometime after that.
(Night will be extended if a replacement is needed for ostriches.)
[/color]

post_rendered:
The sun was rising on the second day of the adventure for creativity. Many were still thinking about the events of the previous day; and the decision to turn ad333 around. There were, of course, other questions too: Why were there saboteurs trying to stop them? What did they have against Goddess Snow? Are we gonna abandon another person today? Who? Was this forest infinitely large? The group gathered up their belongings.

In the silence, someone let out a muffle of surprise, which brought everyone to them in a quick hurry. And, it didn't take long for the surprise to take over, too, as eleven pairs of eyes stared into the dirt in front of them. Three marks. Scratched in the dirt. Probably purposefully left there to scare everyone else out of their wits, and of course, it did. But the message was clear–this wasn't just a search for creativity. They also had to get rid of the saboteurs, before it was too late. Who knew what the goals of the saboteurs are?

The group was finally about to set out again when they noticed that someone was missing. It was weird how no one noticed or even bothered to count heads until the last second, but everyone's attention had probably been on the ominous marks on the ground. After numerous attempts to determine the missing person's identity, they finally figured out who it was. And along with that uncovered, everyone had a faint idea of what the saboteurs were exactly doing throughout the night, because of course it wasn't just to leave some marks and scare people.

Osmium_ had disappeared over the course of the night. He was a Town Novice Friendly Neighbor.

Not all the questions were answered, but it was concluded that they would consider abandoning another person, perhaps one per day, to hopefully get rid of all the saboteurs. And although the forest seemed endless, everyone concluded that the forest probably had to end at some point. Or at least, hoped. The group of eleven finally set out again.

Day 2 has begun and deadline is July 16th at 9:00 pm Central. Majority (and deadline) hang is enabled.

avanders never WIFOMs this kill if he's scum, like have you seen him play?

kevin could WIFOM the kill, but nah.

snoozing probably doesn't WIFOM the kill after what happened in 224.

Now that I've said all this, I probably can't use it anymore, but oh well.

Additionally, the Osmium kill was just too perfect. If someone else besides Osmium and Scipio had died, I would have been very confused, but guess what? Osmium died.

Osmium has done... nothing this game. He did... nothing in 231.

Osmium has lost his N1 kill appeal, so why did he die? (Oh, and if that was your goal @Osmium, you did it pretty well)

Why did he die instead of Scipio, who's pretty accurate these days?

I'm pretttty pretty sure it's not WIFOM.

And why did Osmium die specifically?

Obviously, it has something to do with what he once was and what he can do. He's also still regarded as a non-suspicious kill by people in general. The reality is that he really is. It doesn't matter how good Osmium is if he does absolutely nothing, he's simply not going to solve the game like this. There's no good reason for mafia to fearkill him.

Why would scum shoot Scipio? What if his reads were wrong?

I know Scipio tends to buss his scumbuddies, he has the TMI problem where all the scummy things his buddies do get enhanced to him. Now, it's possible he's gotten over it, but I don't think so.

So, what I'm going to do is vote violet.

post_rendered:
POE is not based on your scumreads, it's based on your townreads.

You take everyone you're very sure is town, and put them in your towncore.

Everyone else is in your POE, so this includes null people.

You can have a preference for which order you get rid of them, though.

Finally, after another whole new painstaking trek, the group came to the edge of the forest just before nightfall, where the worn path ended and led to the base of a mountain. It was extremely intimidating, and the jagged white peak reached up to the clouds. At lower parts of the mountain, one could only see a few a barren land with only a few pine trees spotting the whole area. Only one stream seemed to trickle from the top of the mountain, which seemed to be formed by the thick layer of snow and ice that lay atop the tall mountain. This clearly wasn't the ideal place to be in, especially with tensions so high, but although there was no clear sign in front of the mountain, everyone could feel it–this was surely the mountain with creativity. This was the mountain the group had to scale in order to complete the task given–to find some creativity for Snow.

Before a few of them would recklessly charge in, a few sensible people told everyone to start finding an okay place to spend the night, seeing that the sun had slowly been creeping down and was almost fully dark. Coming to their senses, everyone rushed this way and that, and finally everyone set up a somewhat decent camp and built a campfire. Everyone sat around it, trying to ignore the ghostly shadows it cast around the trees and did what was agreed on in the morning–vote for another person to send back. With the comfort of the campfire, they reached to a somewhat agreeable conclusion and like before told the selected person to head back.

violetmx12 was told to leave immediately and start the long hike home. They were seen shivering before disappearing into the darkness of the night. They were a Vanilla Townie.

Night 2 Deadline is July 14th at 5:30 pm Central. Day 3 will start sometime after that.

post_rendered:
It had been a tense night for most, and many tossed and turned in their bed before finally falling asleep. When everyone woke up, most had bags under their eyes. The pressure must've been high for everyone, with all the saboteur worries along with hoping they hadn't done anything to upset Goddess Snow. From what they knew, that was relatively easy to accomplish, or at least much easier than trying to fulfill the task given to them. Yet they had all recklessly taken the job, and now it was their duty to fulfill it.

Even with a night of restlessness and no one able to get out of bed without a few struggles, the moment everyone was out of bed and managed to get a clear head again, they were checking to see if anyone was missing.


No one had gone missing.
Day 3 deadline is 6:00 pm Central on July 24th.

The entire day had been spent hiking. At least it was different than the two days prior. With the smaller number there had not been as much arguing as in the forrest, which was nice. Though they still spent quite a bit of time discussing who they were gonna send back down the mountain this evening. They also pondered, What is a creativity?, What does it look like?, and Can I eat it?

Goddess Snow was also pondering, but to a much different question: What could possibly be taking them so long!? She wondered if she chose wrongly and, in her frustration and anger, willed snow to start falling in the area the group to possibly remind them of their goal and threaten them if they couldn't achieve it. After doing what she thought could to quicken the pace of the group, she went back to an aggravated state of mind as her creativity couldn't be reached.

On the mountain, maybe half way up, it began to snow. The # group members didn't think much of it at first. But then the stream they were walking along started to freeze over, and that was when they knew they had to hurry up the adventure for creativity. But before they could take a vote

RadioActive1729 slipped onto the now frozen river. He began to slide down the mountain. RadioActive1729 picked up speed and grew smaller and smaller from the perspective of the rest of the group. They didn't think about it long however, as it started to hail so they rushed into the nearby cave.

RadioActive1729 was a Town Indecisive Roleblocker
It is now Night 3, deadline is July 16th at 9:30 pm Central.
Due to the short phase, I will need any living player who didn't post this day phase to pm me confirmation that they're still playing.

post_rendered:
The group members woke up inside of a cave, dark except for the few torches that blazed, although that really just added to the lasting feeling of gloominess. And, with that feeling of gloominess came a lack of motivation that caused everyone to get snappy and quick to point fingers.

Everyone was very frightened, too. That was clear right from the beginning, after the realization that this journey might not just be a search for creativity, but also by the way the moment everyone was awake they searched for missing people.

And as everyone had feared, someone indeed had gone missing. There were no signs of struggle, no noises even as the victim had been taken away. No one even wanted to imagine what had happened, and no one could stop worrying about this happening to themself too. A few checked their sleeping bags again, and found nothing but their own disappointment. It was hollow and looked like someone had peacefully just went out to get a drink, or something. But everyone knew there was more to the story, and once again the saboteurs had struck, and this time there was no sign. No markings. BobDBuilder321 just was not present despite falling asleep inside the cave. BobDBuilder321 was a Town Motion Detector.

After a bit of preparation to leave, and trying to push the fearful thoughts away, the remaining group members slowly head out of the cave to tackle the day. It had stopped precipitating a while ago, and in the morning mist swirled around, making it hard to see a mere few foots away. The journey up the mountain still looked quite daunting ( from what they could hazily see, which was more than enough ), and the the height of it seemed to scare quite a few people. And as they continued up the mountain, many words were said about who the saboteurs were and what should be done today. They could only hope that they could catch a saboteur today.
Day 4 Begins now and deadline is July 28th at 10:00 am Central.

Everyone looked up one more time, trying to find the reassurance of seeing the peak so close. It offered slight warmth, and although everyone had finally caught one saboteur the day before, he had said nothing and gone away before any interrogation could be done. Any that was attempted failed, and the fact that there were still two more did not help.

Soon enough, they arrived at a barren part of the mountain, covered by snow, only a few branches peeking out here and there, all the leaves fallen off and buried beneath the snow. It was hard to imagine that only a few days ago there had been a warm breeze and that they had actually walked in a forest teeming with life. There were few movements besides themselves, and the wind howled into their ears.

Everyone quickly gathered any wood that they could (which was almost just some small sticks scattered on the ground, and started a fire. In the dim light they last 6 people discussed, warmth blasting their skin while cold came and bit it. It was quick and easy, though, and everyone’s glare turned to one person: [name]. They pointed fingers and accused, and finally they shoved avanders away, shouting harsh words and telling him to go.

Everyone looked up one more time, trying to find the reassurance of seeing the peak so close. It offered slight warmth, and although everyone had finally caught one saboteur the day before, he had said nothing and gone away before any interrogation could be done. Any that was attempted failed, and the fact that there were still two more did not help.

Soon enough, they arrived at a barren part of the mountain, covered by snow, only a few branches peeking out here and there, all the leaves fallen off and buried beneath the snow. It was hard to imagine that only a few days ago there had been a warm breeze and that they had actually walked in a forest teeming with life. There were few movements besides themselves, and the wind howled into their ears.

Everyone quickly gathered any wood that they could (which was almost just some small sticks scattered on the ground, and started a fire. In the dim light they last 6 people discussed, warmth blasting their skin while cold came and bit it. It was quick and easy, though, and everyone’s glare turned to one person: [name]. They pointed fingers and accused, and finally they shoved avanders away, shouting harsh words and telling him to go.

post_rendered:
Everyone woke up abruptly, everyone quick to search for missing people. It had almost become a habit, after all these days, but what was different was there was always constant fear. Even when for one night no one had gone missing, it didn't calm down anyone.

Now, when the people searched, they same another sleeping bag empty. And like before, the person who had occupied it had just vanished. Disappeared into thin air. Everyone mourned, but not for long.

vietaformula810 has disappeared during the night. They were a Town Even-Night Watcher.

"Wait..." someone murmured, his eyes flitting around to glance at the others and counting how many people there were, and then frantically to the closest person they could see. He approached them, his eyes shadowed with fear, "There's 4 of us."

That person turned around, and quickly counted the number of people left. "That's true," they mused.

Now he was actually panicking, and stared at water dripping down from a stalagmite, his gaze darkening, turning back towards the person, "T-They equal us in numbers."

"They do." Their voice was oddly calm as they looked at him.

"We have to save ourselves! They might do something bad!" Frantically he looked around for a way to escape, his eyes lighting up when he saw the faint gleam of light that meant the exit out. It wasn't a matter of helping Goddess Snow anymore, it was a matter of saving their own life. If they didn't...

They took a step forward, blocking their path and called, "kevinmathz! Help me over here!"

Someone rushed over, and he stared at the two people who were now surrounding him, "RoyalCactus, come over here! We have to get out of this!"

The people who had still been mourning rushed over, and looked at the scene in shock. "It's you all along."

One of them let out a laugh, "Why not? You all have always been very stupid to trust us. It's been fun seeing everyone in mess, and besides, we have to settle our grudge with the Goddess Snow."

"Oh, yeah," another cut in, "it's been wonderful ruining and sabotaging her mission, and it will just be lovely to finish the job here."

"Don’t…” They all pleaded, heart racing with panic. It might be the end here. Not only would their mission be a fail… It caused a shudder just to think about it. Surviving a fate similar to what happened to everyone else who disappeared.

Of course, any pleading by now would be useless. In the dark, no one could be sure what had been held out, but everyone all agreed: each saboteur took out something and advanced on them, and someone sworn they saw the glint of steel.

Luckily, someone was quick enough to react and nimbly dodged around the advancers, quickly shouting towards the others to follow him as he ran out of the cave. As predicted, the saboteurs started to chance him, and that gave the chance for the others to slip away, too. After they left the cave, though, the saboteurs knew it was impossible to catch them.

“Just let them go,” one grunted annoyingly, his eyes on the footprints on the snow, trying to track where they went anyways, “we’ve done more than enough.”

They all nodded in agreement, and one commented wryly, “Plus, after the all-and-mighty Goddess Snow realizes that they’re mission had been a fail, I’m quite certain their fate won’t be too lucky.

A sudden sweep of cold wind blasted at them, but he just laughed, “Trying to freeze us with that wind? Pathetic. Don’t you have something better up your sleeve?”

A low rumbling came from the other side of the mountain, and he just scoffed, “Why not just kill us, and stop trying to control your temper? It’s too short. And wow so dangerous.”

They all stood there for a second, before snorting, “Nope. Still too afraid. C’mon, let’s get to the top. Destroy that creativity and never let her find it again.”
Congratulations to town for getting bonus flavor ;D

Congratulations to Mafia [Scipio1 (Mafia 2-Shot Jailkeeper), kevinmathz (Mafia Goon), and juliankuang (Mafia Goon)] for winning!!

You may now post.
